<SYSTEM>This is the full developer documentation for conform-ed</SYSTEM>

# conform-ed

> All your conformance are belong to us.

conform-ed gives engineers **typed contracts** to produce standards-correct data, a **headless QTI runtime** to deliver assessments, and **conformance runners** to prove an implementation conforms — across xAPI, QTI, LTI 1.3, Common Cartridge, OneRoster, CASE, CLR, Open Badges, Caliper, and more.

Standards & contracts

Zod validators for \~13 standards in `@conform-ed/contracts`. [Browse the standards →](/contracts/)

QTI delivery runtime

Headless, accessible QTI 3 delivery with `@conform-ed/qti-react` and `qti-xml`. [Deliver QTI →](/qti/)

Conformance runners

Verify xAPI, cmi5, and LTI 1.3 implementations with the runner images. [Run the suites →](/runners/)

Built for AI assistants

An `llms.txt` ships with every build. [Point your assistant at the docs →](/start/ai-assistants/)

# RubricBlockView

Defined in: [test/types.ts:123](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L123)

A rubric block (§4.2.4) carried on the test/part/section view: view-restricted rich content (instructions, scoring guidance) shown to the audiences named in `view`. The block round-trips structurally — any node fields beyond these (printedVariable, stylesheets, catalogInfo) pass through the content converter unchanged.

## Properties

[Section titled “Properties”](#properties)

[Section titled “content?”](#content)

***

[Section titled “view”](#view)

# RubricBlockSchema

> **RubricBlockSchema** = `z.infer`<*typeof* [`rubricBlockSchema`](/api/qti-react/headless/variables/rubricblockschema/)>

Defined in: [test/schema.ts:145](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/schema.ts#L145)

# rubricBlockSchema

> `const` **rubricBlockSchema**: `ZodObject`<{ `content`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodArray`<`ZodUnknown`>>; `use`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodString`>; `view`: `ZodArray`<`ZodString`>; }, `$strip`>

Defined in: [test/schema.ts:139](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/schema.ts#L139)

`rubricBlock` (§4.2.4) — view-restricted rich content at test/part/section level. The `view` audiences are mirrored; `content` is accepted opaquely (the item runtime’s BodyNode model, not authored at the test level), exactly like `testFeedback`.

# assessmentTestDocumentFromView

> **assessmentTestDocumentFromView**(`view`): `unknown`

Defined in: [normalized-item.ts:898](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/normalized-item.ts#L898)

Project an `AssessmentTestView` back to the normalized `qti-assessment-test` document `serializeQtiAssessmentTest` consumes — the inverse of `assessmentTestViewFromNormalized`. Authoring systems that hold a view (or a view-shaped authoring structure) use this to export QTI XML without re-implementing the serializer.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### view

[Section titled “view”](#view)

[`AssessmentTestView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/assessmenttestview/)

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`unknown`

# assessmentTestViewFromNormalized

Reshape a normalized QTI document into the Test Controller’s `AssessmentTestView`, or null when it is not an assessment test.

# collectInteractionConstraints

> **collectInteractionConstraints**(`content`): [`InteractionConstraint`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/interactionconstraint/)\[]

Defined in: [response-validity.ts:44](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-validity.ts#L44)

Walk the item body and collect the constraint attributes its interactions carry. Zero bounds impose nothing: “If max-choices is 0 then there is no restriction”; “If min-choices is 0 then the candidate is not required to select any choices.”

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### content

[Section titled “content”](#content)

readonly [`BodyNode`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/bodynode/)\[] | `undefined`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`InteractionConstraint`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/interactionconstraint/)\[]

# createPackagePciCatalog

> **createPackagePciCatalog**(`files`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`PciCatalog`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/pcicatalog/)>

Defined in: [pci/package.ts:139](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/package.ts#L139)

Build a hash-pinned `PciCatalog` from an (already-unzipped) QTI/CC package file map — `packageRelativePath → bytes`. The catalog resolves the package’s PCI module ids to their in-package bytes, integrity-pinned to the `sha256` computed here; its `fetchBytes` serves those bytes from memory (no network). Inject it into `createPciModuleRegistry({ paths, fetchText })` with **no `baseUrl`** — the package paths are already fully resolved.

Module paths are resolved relative to the package root (where `module_resolution.js` declares them). A declared module whose bytes are absent from the map, or that points at an absolute URL, is omitted — the package vouches only for the bytes it ships.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### files

[Section titled “files”](#files)

`Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `Uint8Array`>>

### options?

[Section titled “options?”](#options)

[`PackagePciCatalogOptions`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/packagepcicatalogoptions/) = `{}`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Promise`<[`PciCatalog`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/pcicatalog/)>

# effectiveItemScore

> **effectiveItemScore**(`scores`, `outcomes`): [`EffectiveItemScore`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/effectiveitemscore/)

Defined in: [item-score.ts:23](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/item-score.ts#L23)

The item score of record (QTI): a numeric SCORE outcome from response processing is authoritative — PCI/RP-scored items (e.g. math-entry) have no per-variable correctResponse basis, so their standard scores read 0. Summed per-variable standard scoring is the fallback for items without RP. MAXSCORE follows the same precedence.

Pure and framework-light: client and server (authoritative finalize) share it so the grade of record is derived identically on both sides.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### scores

[Section titled “scores”](#scores)

readonly [`ScoreResult`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/scoreresult/)\[]

### outcomes

[Section titled “outcomes”](#outcomes)

`Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `unknown`>>

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`EffectiveItemScore`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/effectiveitemscore/)

# foldString

> **foldString**(`value`): `string`

Defined in: [response-processing.ts:21](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-processing.ts#L21)

Lowercase + strip combining diacritics. Exported as a ready-made Response Normalization for language-learning leniency (“cafe” ≈ “Café”) — a documented deviation a consumer must opt into.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### value

[Section titled “value”](#value)

`string`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

# GapMatchReferenceSkin

> **GapMatchReferenceSkin**(`props`): `ReactNode`

# makeAssessmentTestSchema

> **makeAssessmentTestSchema**<`ItemRef`>(`itemRefSchema`): [`AssessmentTestSchemas`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/assessmenttestschemas/)<`output`<`ItemRef`>>

Defined in: [test/schema.ts:252](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/schema.ts#L252)

Build the recursive `assessmentTest` schema around a caller-supplied itemRef schema. Sections nest sections-or-itemRefs through `children` (recursion via `z.lazy`); `z.infer` follows the recursion and carries the injected itemRef’s inferred type. The explicit return type keeps the package’s `isolatedDeclarations` build able to emit the bundle.

## Type Parameters

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](#type-parameters)

### ItemRef

[Section titled “ItemRef”](#itemref)

`ItemRef` *extends* `ZodType`<`unknown`, `unknown`, `$ZodTypeInternals`<`unknown`, `unknown`>>

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### itemRefSchema

[Section titled “itemRefSchema”](#itemrefschema)

`ItemRef`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`AssessmentTestSchemas`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/assessmenttestschemas/)<`output`<`ItemRef`>>

# mapResponse

> **mapResponse**(`declaration`, `response`, `normalize?`): `number`

Defined in: [response-processing.ts:161](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-processing.ts#L161)

`map_response`: sum the mapped values of the response’s members, each member mapped at most once. Per spec, entries default to caseSensitive=true; `caseSensitive: false` lowercases both sides. A Response Normalization, when configured, applies on top for string base types. Applies the mapping’s `defaultValue` to unmatched members and clamps to \[lowerBound, upperBound]. Returns 0 when no mapping exists.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### declaration

[Section titled “declaration”](#declaration)

[`ResponseDeclarationView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/responsedeclarationview/)

### response

[Section titled “response”](#response)

[`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/)

### normalize?

[Section titled “normalize?”](#normalize)

[`ResponseNormalization`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsenormalization/)

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`number`

# mapResponsePoint

> **mapResponsePoint**(`declaration`, `response`): `number`

Defined in: [response-processing.ts:211](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-processing.ts#L211)

`map_response_point`: sum the mapped values of the areas hit by the response’s point members. Per spec each area counts at most once regardless of how many points land in it; points hitting no area add the mapping’s `defaultValue`. Clamps to \[lowerBound, upperBound]. Returns 0 when no areaMapping exists.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### declaration

[Section titled “declaration”](#declaration)

[`ResponseDeclarationView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/responsedeclarationview/)

### response

[Section titled “response”](#response)

[`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/)

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`number`

# matchCorrect

> **matchCorrect**(`declaration`, `response`, `normalize?`): `boolean`

Defined in: [response-processing.ts:101](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-processing.ts#L101)

`match_correct`: true when the response exactly matches `correctResponse`, respecting cardinality and baseType. Spec-strict: no case or diacritic folding unless the consumer passes a Response Normalization. Returns false when no correctResponse exists.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### declaration

[Section titled “declaration”](#declaration)

[`ResponseDeclarationView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/responsedeclarationview/)

### response

[Section titled “response”](#response)

[`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/)

### normalize?

[Section titled “normalize?”](#normalize)

[`ResponseNormalization`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsenormalization/)

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`boolean`

# parsePciModuleResolution

> **parsePciModuleResolution**(`source`): `Record`<`string`, `string`>

Defined in: [pci/package.ts:91](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/package.ts#L91)

Parse a PCI `module_resolution.js` into its `{ moduleId → path }` map. Handles the 1EdTech examples’ pure-JSON form and the RequireJS wrappers (`var require = {…}`, `require.config({…})`) by extracting the config object and JSON-parsing it — so it reads the file as data without executing it. Object literals with unquoted keys (non-JSON) are not parsed; their modules simply do not resolve (and fail loudly at mount) rather than being silently mis-read.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### source

[Section titled “source”](#source)

`string`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Record`<`string`, `string`>

# resolveCatalogSupports

> **resolveCatalogSupports**(`options`): [`CatalogResolution`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/catalogresolution/)

Defined in: [pnp.ts:299](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pnp.ts#L299)

Resolve every catalog’s active alternative content for a candidate. `activeSupports` is the delivery-engine channel — program settings and candidate-toggled options (“or an assessment program’s settings”, §5.28); prohibited supports stay out even when named there.

# resolvePnpActivation

> **resolvePnpActivation**(`pnp`): [`PnpActivation`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/pnpactivation/)

Defined in: [pnp.ts:152](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pnp.ts#L152)

Resolve the activation policy: prohibit-set wins; activate-at-initialization-set is active; activate-as-option-set is offered but off. A preference stated outside any set (e.g. a bare keyword-translation) is honored from the start — the PNP records the need, and without an activation policy there is nothing to defer to (designed policy, see the ADR).

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### pnp

[Section titled “pnp”](#pnp)

[`PnpView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/pnpview/) | `undefined`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`PnpActivation`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/pnpactivation/)

# sanitizeAttributes

> **sanitizeAttributes**(`model`, `elementName`, `attributes`): `Record`<`string`, `string`>

Defined in: [content-model.ts:200](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/content-model.ts#L200)

Reduce a raw attribute bag to the safe, allowlisted subset for one element. Used by the body walk so a node that validates against QTI structure still cannot carry script or handlers. The allowlist is the global set plus the element’s own entries.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### model

[Section titled “model”](#model)

[`ContentModel`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/contentmodel/)

`string`

### attributes

[Section titled “attributes”](#attributes)

`Record`<`string`, `unknown`> | `undefined`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Record`<`string`, `string`>

# scoreResponse

> **scoreResponse**(`declaration`, `response`, `normalize?`): [`ScoreResult`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/scoreresult/)

Defined in: [response-processing.ts:251](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-processing.ts#L251)

Apply the appropriate standard template: `map_response_point` when an areaMapping is declared, `map_response` when a mapping is declared, otherwise `match_correct`. `maxScore` is the mapping upper bound (or the sum of positive mapped values) for mapped items, else 1 for match\_correct. This heuristic backs the per-interaction feedback chrome; item outcomes of record come from the RP interpreter when the item declares `responseProcessing`.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### declaration

[Section titled “declaration”](#declaration)

[`ResponseDeclarationView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/responsedeclarationview/)

### response

[Section titled “response”](#response)

[`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/)

### normalize?

[Section titled “normalize?”](#normalize)

[`ResponseNormalization`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsenormalization/)

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`ScoreResult`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/scoreresult/)

# stimulusContentFromNormalized

> **stimulusContentFromNormalized**(`document`): [`StimulusContentView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/stimuluscontentview/) | `null`

Defined in: [normalized-item.ts:482](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/normalized-item.ts#L482)

The renderable body of a normalized AssessmentStimulus document, for `QtiRuntimeConfig.resolveStimulus`. Returns null for non-stimulus documents.

## Parameters

# AssessmentSectionNode

Defined in: [test/schema.ts:196](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/schema.ts#L196)

The recursive section shape, parameterized by the injected itemRef’s inferred type. Optionals carry `| undefined` and arrays are `readonly` so this matches zod’s object output exactly — letting the `z.lazy` section schema below be annotated `z.ZodType<…>` (a getter cannot self-infer once a generic itemRef joins the `children` union).

#### Inherited from

***

### durationSeconds

[Section titled “durationSeconds”](#durationseconds)

> `readonly` **durationSeconds**: `number` | `null`

Defined in: [store.ts:52](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L52)

Elapsed session seconds at the latest submit (the built-in `duration` response variable handed to RP); null before the first submit. Persist it alongside the responses for server-side replay parity (ADR-0004).

#### Inherited from

[Section titled “Inherited from”](#inherited-from-2)

[`AttemptSnapshot`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/attemptsnapshot/).[`durationSeconds`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/attemptsnapshot/#durationseconds)

***

### interactionStates

[Section titled “interactionStates”](#interactionstates)

> `readonly` **interactionStates**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `string`>>

Defined in: [store.ts:72](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L72)

Per-interaction opaque UI state captured at `suspend()`, keyed by response identifier — currently the `getState()` of in-progress PCI instances (ADR-0012). Persist it with the responses; a resumed session re-creates the store with these as `initialInteractionStates`, and the PCI host re-mounts each instance with its `state` so in-progress custom interactions survive navigation. Empty when no interaction holds suspendable state.

#### Inherited from

[Section titled “Inherited from”](#inherited-from-3)

[`AttemptSnapshot`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/attemptsnapshot/).[`interactionStates`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/attemptsnapshot/#interactionstates)

***

### correctResponses

***

### durationSeconds

[Section titled “durationSeconds”](#durationseconds)

> `readonly` **durationSeconds**: `number` | `null`

Defined in: [store.ts:52](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L52)

Elapsed session seconds at the latest submit (the built-in `duration` response variable handed to RP); null before the first submit. Persist it alongside the responses for server-side replay parity (ADR-0004).

***

### interactionStates

[Section titled “interactionStates”](#interactionstates)

> `readonly` **interactionStates**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `string`>>

Defined in: [store.ts:72](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L72)

Per-interaction opaque UI state captured at `suspend()`, keyed by response identifier — currently the `getState()` of in-progress PCI instances (ADR-0012). Persist it with the responses; a resumed session re-creates the store with these as `initialInteractionStates`, and the PCI host re-mounts each instance with its `state` so in-progress custom interactions survive navigation. Empty when no interaction holds suspendable state.

***

### outcomes

[Section titled “outcomes”](#outcomes)

> `readonly` **outcomes**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`OutcomeValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/outcomevalue/)>>

Defined in: [store.ts:42](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L42)

Item outcomes of record from the RP interpreter; empty before submit or without RP.

***

### responses

[Section titled “responses”](#responses)

> `readonly` **responses**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/)>>

***

### responseViolations

[Section titled “responseViolations”](#responseviolations)

> `readonly` **responseViolations**: readonly [`InteractionConstraint`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/interactionconstraint/)\[]

Defined in: [store.ts:58](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L58)

Interaction constraints the current responses fail (see response-validity). Always visible so UIs can explain themselves; submission is blocked on them only under `validateResponses`.

***

### scores

[Section titled “scores”](#scores)

> `readonly` **scores**: readonly [`ScoreResult`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/scoreresult/)\[]

Per-response heuristic results backing the per-interaction feedback chrome.

### registerResponseCollector

[Section titled “registerResponseCollector”](#registerresponsecollector)

> `readonly` **registerResponseCollector**: (`responseIdentifier`, `collector`) => () => `void`

Defined in: [store.ts:133](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L133)

Imperative interactions (PCI) hold their response internally; a collector pulls it at submit time, before scoring. Returning undefined leaves the response unchanged. Returns the unregister function.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

##### responseIdentifier

[Section titled “responseIdentifier”](#responseidentifier)

`string`

##### collector

[Section titled “collector”](#collector)

() => [`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/) | `undefined`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-1)

() => `void`

***

### registerStateCollector

[Section titled “registerStateCollector”](#registerstatecollector)

> `readonly` **registerStateCollector**: (`responseIdentifier`, `collector`) => () => `void`

Defined in: [store.ts:143](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L143)

Register a `suspend()`-time state collector for an imperative interaction that owns opaque UI state (PCI `getState()`). On suspend the store pulls every collector into `AttemptSnapshot.interactionStates`; returning undefined records no state for that interaction. Returns the unregister function.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters-1)

##### responseIdentifier

[Section titled “responseIdentifier”](#responseidentifier-1)

`string`

##### collector

[Section titled “collector”](#collector-1)

() => `string` | `undefined`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-2)

() => `void`

***

### suspend

[Section titled “suspend”](#suspend)

> `readonly` **suspend**: () => `void`

Defined in: [store.ts:152](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L152)

Stop the session clock: duration “records the accumulated time (in seconds) of all Candidate Sessions for all Attempts … minus any time the session was in the suspended state”. Navigating away from an item suspends its session (spec); the test session store drives this. Idempotent.

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-8)

`void`

# AttemptStoreOptions

Defined in: [store.ts:81](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L81)

Consumer-supplied input. Optional members deliberately admit explicit `undefined` (“undefined means not provided”), so callers can pass maybe-undefined values straight through; the store reads every member field-wise (`??`/`?.`) and never spread-merges options over defaults.

### initialInteractionStates?

[Section titled “initialInteractionStates?”](#initialinteractionstates)

> `readonly` `optional` **initialInteractionStates?**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `string`>>

Restore baseline for per-interaction suspendable UI state (a prior session’s `AttemptSnapshot.interactionStates`), keyed by response identifier. The PCI host reads its entry from the snapshot and re-mounts the instance with that `state`.

The Response Normalization hook (ADR-0004); applies to scores and outcomes alike.

***

### now?

[Section titled “now?”](#now)

> `readonly` `optional` **now?**: () => `number`

Defined in: [store.ts:105](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/store.ts#L105)

Millisecond clock backing the built-in `duration` response variable (wall-clock from session start to submit). Injectable for deterministic tests and replays; defaults to Date.now.

### validateResponses?

> `readonly` `optional` **validateResponses?**: `boolean`

ItemSessionControl validate-responses: “candidates are not allowed to submit the item until they have provided valid responses for all interactions”. When set, submit() refuses while `responseViolations` is non-empty.

### renderContent

[Section titled “renderContent”](#rendercontent)

> **renderContent**: (`nodes`, `overrides?`) => `ReactNode`

Defined in: [runtime.ts:174](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L174)

Render body fragments (prompt, choice labels) through the core allowlist walk. `overrides` lets a skin take over specific node kinds nested anywhere in the fragment (e.g. `hottext`, `gap`) while the core keeps walking everything else.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters-4)

##### nodes

`ReactNode`

# InterpolationTableEntryView

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:53](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L53)

One interpolationTable row; sourceValue is “the lower bound … to match this entry” (§7.18.1).

## Properties

[Section titled “includeBoundary?”](#includeboundary)

“If ‘true’, the default, then an exact match of the value is considered a match of this entry.” (§7.18.2)

# InterpolationTableView

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:66](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L66)

“An interpolationTable transforms a source float (or integer) by finding the first interpolationTableEntry with a sourceValue that is less than or equal to (subject to includeBoundary) the source value.” (§5.78)

***

### interactionSchemas?

[Section titled “interactionSchemas?”](#interactionschemas)

> `readonly` `optional` **interactionSchemas?**: `ReadonlyMap`<`string`, `ZodType`<`unknown`, `unknown`, `$ZodTypeInternals`<`unknown`, `unknown`>>>

Defined in: [item-capability.ts:64](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/item-capability.ts#L64)

Optional per-kind schemas for the stricter `invalid-interaction` check. The React runtime supplies its descriptor schemas; a headless caller that has already validated structure (e.g. against the qti-xml contracts schema) can omit them.

***

### model?

Content model deciding the flow-element allowlist + math root; defaults to v0.

# ItemRendererProps

Defined in: [runtime.ts:243](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L243)

## Properties

### activeSupports?

[Section titled “activeSupports?”](#activesupports)

> `optional` **activeSupports?**: readonly `string`\[]

Defined in: [runtime.ts:278](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L278)

Supports in effect beyond the PNP’s initial activation — program settings and candidate-toggled options (activate-as-option-set). Prohibited supports stay out.

***

### children?

[Section titled “children?”](#children)

> `optional` **children?**: `ReactNode`

Defined in: [runtime.ts:282](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L282)

***

### item

[Section titled “item”](#item)

> **item**: [`AssessmentItemView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/assessmentitemview/)

Defined in: [runtime.ts:244](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L244)

***

### mode?

[Section titled “mode?”](#mode)

> `optional` **mode?**: [`ItemRenderMode`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/itemrendermode/)

Defined in: [runtime.ts:260](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L260)

The item-session state to render. `review` is read-only: “the candidate can review the qti-item-body along with the responses they gave, but cannot update or resubmit them”. `solution` additionally swaps in the clone’s resolved correct responses (“a way of entering the solution state”); the show-solution gate is the consumer’s (effective ItemSessionControl). Default: “interact”.

***

### pnp?

[Section titled “pnp?”](#pnp)

> `optional` **pnp?**: [`PnpView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/pnpview/)

Defined in: [runtime.ts:273](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L273)

The candidate’s AfA PNP. Activates the item’s dormant catalog supports (§5.29): “A candidate’s profile (or assessment program settings) will indicate whether the candidate should be presented any of the possible supports.”

***

### seed?

[Section titled “seed?”](#seed)

> `optional` **seed?**: `number`

Defined in: [runtime.ts:252](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L252)

Clone seed for template processing; store it to replay the same clone.

***

### showFeedback?

[Section titled “showFeedback?”](#showfeedback)

> `optional` **showFeedback?**: `boolean`

Defined in: [runtime.ts:267](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L267)

Effective ItemSessionControl show-feedback; consulted only outside `interact`. `false` withholds modal and integrated feedback — visibility is then “determined by the default values of the outcome variables” — and is ignored for adaptive items, per spec.

***

### store?

[Section titled “store?”](#store)

> `optional` **store?**: [`AttemptStore`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/attemptstore/)

Defined in: [runtime.ts:250](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L250)

An externally owned attempt store. Without it the renderer creates a fresh per-mount store; passing one enables review/replay modes (rehydrate a stored, already-submitted attempt) and server-side rendering of submitted states.

# ItemSessionControlView

Defined in: [test/types.ts:24](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L24)

QTI `itemSessionControl`: per-level overrides cascading testPart → section → itemRef. The controller enforces `maxAttempts` and `allowSkipping`; the rest is surfaced for delivery chrome (review/solution/comment affordances are UI concerns).

# PciCatalog

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:42](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L42)

## Properties

[Section titled “Properties”](#properties)

### fetchText

[Section titled “fetchText”](#fetchtext)

> `readonly` **fetchText**: (`url`) => `Promise`<`string`>

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:50](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L50)

The allowlist- and integrity-enforcing source fetcher for `createPciModuleRegistry({ fetchText })`. Rejects any URL not in the catalog and any source whose `sha256` does not match the pinned integrity.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

##### url

[Section titled “url”](#url)

`string`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Promise`<`string`>

***

### has

[Section titled “has”](#has)

> `readonly` **has**: (`moduleId`) => `boolean`

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:56](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L56)

Whether a content-declared module id is a vetted, deliverable catalog entry — the deliverability predicate a consumer’s ingest gate consults (ADR-0012: flip a PCI item from quarantined to deliverable exactly when its module resolves here).

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters-1)

##### moduleId

[Section titled “moduleId”](#moduleid)

`string`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-1)

***

### paths

[Section titled “paths”](#paths)

> `readonly` **paths**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `string`>>

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:44](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L44)

module id → vetted URL, for `createPciModuleRegistry({ paths })`.

# PciCatalogEntry

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:19](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L19)

A hash-pinned PCI module catalog: a *policy wrapper* over the registry’s existing `paths` + `fetchText` seam (it adds no loader). It turns “deliver whatever the content’s `module_resolution` points at” — content-author / arbitrary-internet JavaScript evaluated in a learner’s browser, which ADR-0006/0007 refused as a default — into an explicit, verifiable publishing decision:

* **Allowlist (default-deny).** Only module ids the catalog vetted resolve, and only to their vetted URLs. The registry asking for any other URL is refused loudly (ADR-0003), never blind-eval’d.
* **Integrity.** Fetched source is checked against a pinned `sha256` *before* it is handed to the registry to `evaluate`. A swapped/tampered module fails closed.

Inject the result into `createPciModuleRegistry({ paths: catalog.paths, fetchText: catalog.fetchText })`; the registry and `mountPci` are otherwise unchanged. Trust becomes a publishing decision (ADR-0006) instead of “whatever the content points at”.

## Properties

[Section titled “Properties”](#properties)

### integrity

[Section titled “integrity”](#integrity)

> `readonly` **integrity**: `string`

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:28](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L28)

The pinned `sha256` of the module source: either a bare hex digest or an SRI `sha256-<base64>` string. Normalized to lowercase hex before comparison, so an emergent asset row’s server-authoritative `sha256` is a catalog entry verbatim (ADR-0012 — one hash, computed once at finalize).

***

### url

[Section titled “url”](#url)

> `readonly` **url**: `string`

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:21](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L21)

The vetted URL this module id resolves to (and the sole allowlist member for it).

module id → vetted entry. The set of entry URLs *is* the fetch allowlist.

### fetchBytes?

[Section titled “fetchBytes?”](#fetchbytes)

> `readonly` `optional` **fetchBytes?**: (`url`) => `Promise`<`Uint8Array`<`ArrayBufferLike`>>

Defined in: [pci/catalog.ts:39](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/catalog.ts#L39)

Byte fetcher for vetted URLs; defaults to global `fetch` → `arrayBuffer`. The consumer injects its own (e.g. emergent’s signed-URL fetch). Only ever called for an allowlisted URL — the allowlist check runs first.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

[Section titled “url”](#url)

`string`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Promise`<`Uint8Array`<`ArrayBufferLike`>>

# PciModuleRegistryOptions

# QtiRuntime

Defined in: [runtime.ts:295](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L295)

### canDeliver

[Section titled “canDeliver”](#candeliver)

> **canDeliver**: (`item`) => [`CapabilityReport`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/capabilityreport/)

Defined in: [runtime.ts:316](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L316)

The Capability Report for an item against this runtime’s injected descriptors, skins, and content model. Consumers gate delivery on it (ADR-0003); the ItemRenderer placeholder is only the backstop for content that reaches rendering anyway.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

##### item

[Section titled “item”](#item)

[`AssessmentItemView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/assessmentitemview/)

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`CapabilityReport`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/capabilityreport/)

Flow content outside an item attempt — test feedback, rubric copy. Same sanitizer and node walk as the item body, over caller-supplied outcome values.

### useCatalogSupports

[Section titled “useCatalogSupports”](#usecatalogsupports)

> **useCatalogSupports**: (`catalogIdref`) => readonly [`ResolvedCatalogSupport`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/resolvedcatalogsupport/)\[]

Defined in: [runtime.ts:309](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L309)

The active supports resolved for a catalog idref — for skins whose own nodes carry data-catalog-idref (e.g. a choice label) and consumers building support UI (glossary panels, toggles). The core walk already decorates generic flow and block nodes; this is the same resolution by hand.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters-1)

##### catalogIdref

[Section titled “catalogIdref”](#catalogidref)

`string` | `undefined`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-2)

readonly [`ResolvedCatalogSupport`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/resolvedcatalogsupport/)\[]

> `readonly` `optional` **assetResolver?**: (`href`) => `string`

### customOperators?

[Section titled “customOperators?”](#customoperators)

> `readonly` `optional` **customOperators?**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`CustomOperatorImplementation`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/customoperatorimplementation/)>>

Defined in: [runtime.ts:227](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L227)

Registered vendor `customOperator` implementations by class. Items using only registered classes pass the capability gate; everything else stays unsupported.

### interactions

### renderCatalogSupport?

[Section titled “renderCatalogSupport?”](#rendercatalogsupport)

> `readonly` `optional` **renderCatalogSupport?**: (`support`, `catalogIdref`) => `ReactNode`

Defined in: [runtime.ts:240](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L240)

Replaces the default rendering of an active catalog support (the note-role span appended beside the referenced content). The delivery engine owns presentation — tooltips, players, glossary panels — the runtime owns resolution.

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters-1)

##### support

[Section titled “support”](#support)

[`ResolvedCatalogSupport`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/resolvedcatalogsupport/)

##### catalogIdref

[Section titled “catalogIdref”](#catalogidref)

`string`

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-1)

`ReactNode`

***

Replaces the default Unsupported Placeholder for interaction nodes this runtime cannot render.

### resolveStimulus?

[Section titled “resolveStimulus?”](#resolvestimulus)

> `readonly` `optional` **resolveStimulus?**: (`ref`) => [`StimulusContentView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/stimuluscontentview/) | `null`

Defined in: [runtime.ts:234](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/runtime.ts#L234)

Resolve an item’s shared-stimulus reference to its normalized body content (synchronous by design, like the session store’s resolveItem: load the package’s stimuli before mounting; `stimulusContentFromNormalized` reshapes a normalized document). Unresolved refs are capability issues, never silent drops (ADR-0003).

#### Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters-3)

##### ref

[Section titled “ref”](#ref)

[`AssessmentStimulusRefView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/assessmentstimulusrefview/)

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns-3)

[`StimulusContentView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/stimuluscontentview/) | `null`

***

# ResponseProcessingContext

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:197](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L197)

Consumer-supplied input. Optional members deliberately admit explicit `undefined` (“undefined means not provided”): callers pass maybe-undefined values straight through, and the interpreter reads every member field-wise (`??`/`?.`) — option bags are never spread-merged over defaults.

## Properties

[Section titled “Properties”](#properties)

### completionStatus?

[Section titled “completionStatus?”](#completionstatus)

> `readonly` `optional` **completionStatus?**: `string`

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:226](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L226)

The session’s current `completionStatus` — the third built-in, “declared implicitly”; it enters the outcome map (defaulting to “not\_attempted”) so `setOutcomeValue` can change it (§2.2.2.3). An explicit declaration (legacy content) wins over this value.

***

### customOperators?

[Section titled “customOperators?”](#customoperators)

> `readonly` `optional` **customOperators?**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`CustomOperatorImplementation`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/customoperatorimplementation/)>>

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:228](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L228)

Registered vendor `customOperator` implementations by class (opt-in).

***

### duration?

[Section titled “duration?”](#duration)

> `readonly` `optional` **duration?**: `number`

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:218](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L218)

Built-in session variables (reserved identifiers; items must not declare them). `duration` is the item session’s elapsed seconds; `numAttempts` “increases by 1 at the start of each attempt”, so it includes the attempt being scored.

[Section titled “priorOutcomes?”](#prioroutcomes)

> `readonly` `optional` **priorOutcomes?**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`OutcomeValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/outcomevalue/)>>

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:206](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L206)

Adaptive carry-over: outcome values from earlier attempts replace declared defaults.

***

### random?

[Section titled “random?”](#random)

> `readonly` `optional` **random?**: () => `number`

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:212](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L212)

Random source for `random`/`randomInteger`/`randomFloat` in RP (adaptive items, e.g. Monty Hall door reveals). Seed it from the attempt seed: the seed plus the submission sequence then replays the exact same outcomes (ADR-0004 determinism).

#### Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`number`

***

***

### correct?

[Section titled “correct?”](#correct)

> `readonly` `optional` **correct?**: `boolean`

Whether every scorable response variable matched (feeds numberCorrect / numberIncorrect). Omit when the item has nothing to be correct about.

***

### durationSeconds?

[Section titled “durationSeconds?”](#durationseconds)

> `readonly` `optional` **durationSeconds?**: `number`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:223](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L223)

The item session’s elapsed seconds (`AttemptSnapshot.durationSeconds`). Resolves the built-in `ITEM.duration` in outcome processing; unreported → NULL.

***

> `readonly` `optional` **responded?**: `boolean`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:216](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L216)

The candidate gave at least one non-empty response (feeds numberResponded).

***

### responses?

[Section titled “responses?”](#responses)

> `readonly` `optional` **responses?**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`ResponseValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/responsevalue/)>>

Defined in: [test/types.ts:235](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L235)

The candidate’s responses as submitted — recorded into the attempt history so results reporting can emit `candidateResponse` values from persisted state.

***

### submittedAtMs?

[Section titled “submittedAtMs?”](#submittedatms)

> `readonly` `optional` **submittedAtMs?**: `number`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:240](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L240)

The submission instant (epoch ms). Controller-stamped: callers never set it; it rides pending simultaneous results so the flush keeps submit-time stamps.

***

### valid?

[Section titled “valid?”](#valid)

> `readonly` `optional` **valid?**: `boolean`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:230](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L230)

Whether the responses satisfy the interaction constraints (response-validity). Under effective `validateResponses` in an individual-submission part, `false` makes the controller refuse the submission (“candidates are not allowed to submit the item until they have provided valid responses for all interactions”).

# TestPartNode

Defined in: [test/schema.ts:215](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/schema.ts#L215)

A test part: navigation/submission modes plus the cascade levels and its sections.

## Type Parameters

# TestPlanItem

Defined in: [test/types.ts:162](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L162)

## Properties

[Section titled “Properties”](#properties)

### instance?

[Section titled “instance?”](#instance)

> `readonly` `optional` **instance?**: `number`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:173](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L173)

1-based instantiation number; present only when the ref has multiple instances.

***

### key

[Section titled “key”](#key)

> `readonly` **key**: `string`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:171](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L171)

The session key: the ref identifier, or `identifier.n` when selection with-replacement instantiates the ref more than once — the spec’s own instance addressing, where “a number that denotes the instance’s place in the sequence of the item’s instantiation is inserted between the item variable identifier and the item variable” (§2.11.1.2). Identifiers cannot contain periods, so the two forms never collide.

***

### partIdentifier

[Section titled “partIdentifier”](#partidentifier)

### itemOutcomes

### pendingItemResults

[Section titled “pendingItemResults”](#pendingitemresults)

> `readonly` **pendingItemResults**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`TestItemResult`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/testitemresult/)>>

Defined in: [test/types.ts:317](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L317)

Results held back in simultaneous-submission parts (QTI: the part’s responses are submitted together). They commit when the part is left or the test ends; until then they are invisible to outcome processing and feedback.

***

### presentedItems

### respondedItems

[Section titled “respondedItems”](#respondeditems)

> `readonly` **respondedItems**: readonly `string`\[]

Defined in: [test/types.ts:308](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L308)

Items whose latest attempt carried a response (feeds numberResponded).

***

### status

[Section titled “status”](#status)

> `readonly` **status**: `"in-progress"` | `"suspended"` | `"ended"`

Defined in: [test/types.ts:300](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L300)

`suspended` stops every scope clock and blocks transitions until `resume()` — the gap never accrues to any duration (“minus any time the session was in the suspended state”). Pre-suspension persisted states only ever carry the other two.

***

### templateDefaultValues?

[Section titled “templateDefaultValues?”](#templatedefaultvalues)

> `readonly` `optional` **templateDefaultValues?**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, [`OutcomeValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/outcomevalue/)>>>>

Defined in: [test/types.ts:326](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L326)

Evaluated `templateDefault` values per item key, recorded at the spec’s times (§5.152: linear — when the item first becomes current; nonlinear — at testPart start) so item-store creation reads a stable, replayable value.

***

# TestSessionStore

Defined in: [test/session-store.ts:54](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/session-store.ts#L54)

### assessmentResult

[Section titled “assessmentResult”](#assessmentresult)

> `readonly` **assessmentResult**: (`options?`) => `AssessmentResultDocumentView`

Defined in: [test/session-store.ts:81](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/session-store.ts#L81)

Build the QTI Results Reporting document for the session as it stands: per-attempt final itemResults, pending submissions, initial entries for everything else, typed by the resolved item views and the clones’ correct responses. Serialize with qti-xml’s `serializeQtiAssessmentResult`.

# TestTimingState

Defined in: [test/types.ts:263](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L263)

Wall-clock accounting folded at every controller transition (and `tick()`). Durations are always “as of” `lastTransitionAtMs` — scoring and enforcement read recorded state only (ADR-0004 determinism). Until a suspend/resume API exists, these include all wall time between transitions.

## Properties

[Section titled “Properties”](#properties)

### itemSeconds

[Section titled “itemSeconds”](#itemseconds)

> `readonly` **itemSeconds**: `Readonly`<`Record`<`string`, `number`>>

Defined in: [test/types.ts:276](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L276)

Seconds each item has been the current item — the enforcement clock for item minTime/maxTime. The `ITEM.duration` variable reads the consumer report instead.

# TimeLimitsView

Defined in: [test/types.ts:42](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/test/types.ts#L42)

QTI `timeLimits` (seconds). The controller enforces these under an injectable clock (`TestControllerOptions.now`): expiry checks fold elapsed time at every transition, and consumers drive their own timers by calling `tick()` (ADR-0005, “Timing and time limits”). The values still surface on the plan for consumer-side countdowns.

# CapabilityIssueType

> **CapabilityIssueType** = `"unsupported-interaction"` | `"invalid-interaction"` | `"unsupported-element"` | `"unsupported-rp"`

Defined in: [capability.ts:7](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/capability.ts#L7)

Capability Report types (ADR-0003): the runtime’s answer to “can this content be delivered, and if not, why”. In their own module so the RP interpreter can report issues without importing the React runtime.

# CustomOperatorImplementation

> **CustomOperatorImplementation** = (`args`, `expression`) => [`MaybeRpValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/mayberpvalue/)

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:145](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L145)

A consumer-registered `customOperator` implementation, keyed by its `class` attribute. Receives the already-evaluated child values; returns NULL-or-value like any expression. Vendor operators are by definition engine-specific (the spec leaves them implementation-defined), so nothing ships registered — same opt-in stance as PCI modules.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### args

[Section titled “args”](#args)

readonly [`MaybeRpValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/mayberpvalue/)\[]

### expression

[Section titled “expression”](#expression)

[`RpExpressionView`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/rpexpressionview/)

[`MaybeRpValue`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/mayberpvalue/)

# OutcomeValue

> **OutcomeValue** = [`RpScalar`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/rpscalar/) | readonly [`RpScalar`](/api/qti-react/index/type-aliases/rpscalar/)\[] | `null`

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:15](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L15)

An outcome variable’s value as exposed to consumers after response processing.

# QtiShape

> **QtiShape** = `"circle"` | `"rect"` | `"poly"` | `"ellipse"` | `"default"`

Defined in: [graphic.ts:13](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/graphic.ts#L13)

Graphic primitives shared by the graphic interaction family and areaMapping scoring: QTI shape/coords parsing and point-in-shape hit testing. Pure logic, no React.

Shapes follow the QTI (HTML image-map) conventions:

* `circle`: center-x, center-y, radius
* `rect`: left-x, top-y, right-x, bottom-y
* `poly`: x1, y1, …, xn, yn
* `ellipse`: center-x, center-y, radius-x, radius-y
* `default`: the entire image

# ResponseConstraintKind

> **ResponseConstraintKind** = `"minChoices"` | `"maxChoices"` | `"minAssociations"` | `"maxAssociations"` | `"minStrings"` | `"patternMask"` | `"minPlays"`

Defined in: [response-validity.ts:18](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/response-validity.ts#L18)

# ResponseNormalization

> **ResponseNormalization** = (`value`, `declaration?`) => `string`

Defined in: [rp/types.ts:182](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/rp/types.ts#L182)

Response Normalization (ADR-0004): an opt-in, consumer-configured transform of candidate string input applied at comparison time. Off by default; always off in conformance runs. Applied to both sides of string comparisons so keys and candidate input normalize identically.

# outcomeDeclarationSchema

> `const` **outcomeDeclarationSchema**: `ZodObject`<{ `baseType`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodString`>; `cardinality`: `ZodEnum`<{ `multiple`: `"multiple"`; `ordered`: `"ordered"`; `record`: `"record"`; `single`: `"single"`; }>; `defaultValue`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodObject`<{ `values`: `ZodArray`<`ZodObject`<{ `value`: `ZodUnion`\<readonly \[`ZodString`, `ZodNumber`, `ZodBoolean`]>; }, `$strip`>>; }, `$strip`>>; `identifier`: `ZodString`; `interpolationTable`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodObject`<{ `defaultValue`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodUnion`\<readonly \[`ZodString`, `ZodNumber`, `ZodBoolean`]>>; `interpolationTableEntries`: `ZodArray`<`ZodObject`<{ `includeBoundary`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodBoolean`>; `sourceValue`: `ZodNumber`; `targetValue`: `ZodUnion`\<readonly \[`ZodString`, `ZodNumber`, `ZodBoolean`]>; }, `$strip`>>; }, `$strip`>>; `matchTable`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodObject`<{ `defaultValue`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodUnion`\<readonly \[`ZodString`, `ZodNumber`, `ZodBoolean`]>>; `matchTableEntries`: `ZodArray`<`ZodObject`<{ `sourceValue`: `ZodNumber`; `targetValue`: `ZodUnion`\<readonly \[`ZodString`, `ZodNumber`, `ZodBoolean`]>; }, `$strip`>>; }, `$strip`>>; `normalMaximum`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodNumber`>; `normalMinimum`: `ZodOptional`<`ZodNumber`>; }, `$strip`>

# portableCustomInteraction

> `const` **portableCustomInteraction**: [`InteractionDescriptor`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/interactiondescriptor/)<`"portableCustomInteraction"`>

Defined in: [pci/interaction.ts:35](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/pci/interaction.ts#L35)

The PCI interaction descriptor. Deliberately NOT part of `qtiCoreInteractions`: delivering a PCI executes item-supplied JavaScript, so consumers opt in by adding this descriptor plus a skin from `createPciSkin` (ADR-0003 — the capability gate keeps PCI items undeliverable until then).

# positionObjectStage

> `const` **positionObjectStage**: [`InteractionDescriptor`](/api/qti-react/index/interfaces/interactiondescriptor/)<`"positionObjectStage"`>

The common single-interaction stage; multi-interaction stages fail validation.

# referenceInteractionKinds

> `const` **referenceInteractionKinds**: readonly `string`\[]

Defined in: [item-capability.ts:190](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-react/src/item-capability.ts#L190)

Interaction kinds the bundled reference skin renders — the default “supported set” for callers that deliver with the reference skin. Kept in parity with the reference skin by a test; pass an explicit set to `reportItemCapability` for a custom delivery surface.

string | number | boolean — the QTI scalar value space (NULL is modelled by absence).

# convertCcQtiV1ToV3

> **convertCcQtiV1ToV3**(`xml`, `options?`): [`CcQtiConversionResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/ccqticonversionresult/)

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/cc-qti/convert-to-v3.ts:442](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/cc-qti/convert-to-v3.ts#L442)

Convert a CC `questestinterop` XML string into QTI 3.0.1 artifacts. Returns the converted item XMLs (+ a test XML when the source is an `<assessment>`), or a structured `invalid` result when the input is not even structurally valid CC QTI.

Validation defaults to **structural** (the raw CC schema). The stricter CC *profile* rules (item-type coherence, no duplicate idents, feedback linkage) are opt-in via `{ profile: true }` — they are a conformance gate for clean cartridges, not a bar a best-effort import of a real-world export should trip over (those routinely violate the profile).

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### xml

[Section titled “xml”](#xml)

`string`

### options?

[Section titled “options?”](#options)

#### profile?

[Section titled “profile?”](#profile)

`boolean`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`CcQtiConversionResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/ccqticonversionresult/)

# convertQtiV3AssessmentToCcV1

> **convertQtiV3AssessmentToCcV1**(`input`): [`QtiV3ToCcV1AssessmentResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/qtiv3toccv1assessmentresult/)

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/cc-qti/convert-to-v1.ts:459](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/cc-qti/convert-to-v1.ts#L459)

Convert a set of QTI 3 items into one CC 1.2.1 `<questestinterop>` assessment. Each item is converted independently; the assessment carries only the converted items, and `assessmentXml` is `null` when none could be converted (the producer then degrades the whole assessment to webcontent).

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### input

[Section titled “input”](#input)

#### identifier

[Section titled “identifier”](#identifier)

`string`

#### items

[Section titled “items”](#items)

readonly `object`\[]

#### title

[Section titled “title”](#title)

`string`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`QtiV3ToCcV1AssessmentResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/qtiv3toccv1assessmentresult/)

# convertQtiV3ItemToCcV1

> **convertQtiV3ItemToCcV1**(`xml`, `fallbackIdentifier?`): [`QtiV3ToCcV1ItemResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/qtiv3toccv1itemresult/)

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/cc-qti/convert-to-v1.ts:361](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/cc-qti/convert-to-v1.ts#L361)

Convert one QTI 3 `assessmentItem` to a CC 1.2.1 `<item>`. Returns `unsupported` (never throws on shape) for an item that is not a single choice/text-entry/extended-text interaction, so the producer can render it as a visible fallback rather than silently dropping it.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### xml

[Section titled “xml”](#xml)

`string`

### fallbackIdentifier?

[Section titled “fallbackIdentifier?”](#fallbackidentifier)

`string` = `"item"`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`QtiV3ToCcV1ItemResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/qtiv3toccv1itemresult/)

# escapeText

> **escapeText**(`value`): `string`

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/xml-writer.ts:7](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/xml-writer.ts#L7)

A tiny indenting XML writer shared by the binding serializers (results, usage data, AfA PNP). The XSDs’ sequences dictate emit order; escaping covers text content and attribute values.

# normalizeQuestestinterop

> **normalizeQuestestinterop**(`xml`, `options?`): [`NormalizeQuestestinteropResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/normalizequestestinteropresult/)

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/cc-qti/normalize-questestinterop.ts:505](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/cc-qti/normalize-questestinterop.ts#L505)

Parse + normalize a `questestinterop` XML string into the CC 1.4 contract shape and validate it. When `profile` is true (default), the stricter CC profile rules are applied (item-type coherence, feedback linkage); when false, only the structural raw schema is checked.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### xml

[Section titled “xml”](#xml)

`string`

### options?

[Section titled “options?”](#options)

#### profile?

[Section titled “profile?”](#profile)

`boolean`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

[`NormalizeQuestestinteropResult`](/api/qti-xml/type-aliases/normalizequestestinteropresult/)

# selectQtiSchema

# serializeQtiAccessForAllPnp

> **serializeQtiAccessForAllPnp**(`document`): `string`

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/serialize-pnp.ts:338](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/serialize-pnp.ts#L338)

Serialize a candidate-preferences document against the official QTI 3 profile binding.

# validateQtiPackageArchive

> **validateQtiPackageArchive**(`zipBytes`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`QtiPackageValidationResult`](/api/qti-xml/interfaces/qtipackagevalidationresult/)>

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/validate-package.ts:322](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/validate-package.ts#L322)

Validate a QTI 3 content package held entirely in memory as PIF (Package Interchange Format) ZIP bytes. Only `.xml` entries are decompressed (media is skipped), but the caller’s full byte buffer is still resident — suitable for modest packages and callers that already hold the bytes (tests, small authored packages). For potentially large packages prefer `validateQtiPackagePath`, which streams from disk.

xi:include across archive entries is not resolved on this in-memory path (the resolver reads from the filesystem); `validateQtiPackagePath` resolves them.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### zipBytes

[Section titled “zipBytes”](#zipbytes)

`Uint8Array`

### options?

[Section titled “options?”](#options)

#### reportPath?

[Section titled “reportPath?”](#reportpath)

`string`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Promise`<[`QtiPackageValidationResult`](/api/qti-xml/interfaces/qtipackagevalidationresult/)>

# validateQtiPackagePath

> **validateQtiPackagePath**(`packagePath`): `Promise`<[`QtiPackageValidationResult`](/api/qti-xml/interfaces/qtipackagevalidationresult/)>

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/validate-package.ts:377](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/validate-package.ts#L377)

Validate a QTI 3 content package at a filesystem path: an exploded directory, or a PIF ZIP file. The ZIP route streams from disk, materializing only the package’s XML into a temporary directory (media is never inflated, so memory stays bounded for large packages), then validates the exploded tree — which also resolves xi:include across entries — and cleans the temp directory up afterwards.

## Parameters

[Section titled “Parameters”](#parameters)

### packagePath

[Section titled “packagePath”](#packagepath)

`string`

## Returns

[Section titled “Returns”](#returns)

`Promise`<[`QtiPackageValidationResult`](/api/qti-xml/interfaces/qtipackagevalidationresult/)>

# validateQtiXmlContent

> **validateQtiXmlContent**(`xml`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`QtiValidationResult`](/api/qti-xml/interfaces/qtivalidationresult/)>

Defined in: [qti-xml/src/validate.ts:45](https://github.com/conform-ed/conform-ed/blob/714798ca8a5caa20191f3c24bde2f0dfbbadfe31/packages/qti-xml/src/validate.ts#L45)

Validate an in-memory XML instance (e.g. a serialized assessmentResult on its way out — the export-conformance round trip). `sourcePath` anchors relative xi:include hrefs and the reported `filePath`; in-memory instances default to the cwd.

## Parameters

[Section titled “xml”](#xml)

`string`

### options?

[Section titled “options?”](#options)

#### sourcePath?

[Section titled “sourcePath?”](#sourcepath)

`string`

## Returns

`Promise`<[`QtiValidationResult`](/api/qti-xml/interfaces/qtivalidationresult/)>

# QtiV3ToCcV1ItemResult

CC 1.x carries QTI ASI 1.2.1 under this namespace.

# CLI

> Validate QTI XML files, folders, and packages from the command line.

conform-ed ships command-line helpers for QTI validation, run from the monorepo via Bun scripts:

* **Validate a file** — `bun run qti:validate:file <file.xml>`
* **Validate a folder** — `bun run qti:validate:folder <dir>` (against the currently supported normalization slice)
* **Validate a package** — `bun run qti:validate:package <dir>` (exploded QTI package rooted at `imsmanifest.xml`)
* **Coverage report** — `bun run qti:coverage:report` (separates root-level coverage from actual validation success)
* **Inventory examples** — `bun run qti:inventory:examples`

These commands sit on top of [`@conform-ed/qti-xml`](/qti/). Packaging the CLI for standalone use is on the roadmap; this section will expand as that lands.

# Common Cartridge

> Read and write IMS Common Cartridge packages with @conform-ed/common-cartridge.

`@conform-ed/common-cartridge` works with [IMS Common Cartridge](https://www.imsglobal.org/cc/) packages — the `imsmanifest.xml`, its resources, web content, web links, and the other cartridge surfaces — for both v1.3 and v1.4.

```bash
bun add @conform-ed/common-cartridge
```

It pairs with the Common Cartridge schemas in [`@conform-ed/contracts`](/contracts/) (`@conform-ed/contracts/common-cartridge/v1_3` and `…/v1_4`).

API guides and worked import/export examples are being expanded in this section.

# Standards & contracts

> Typed Zod validators and inferred types for the common digital-education standards, in @conform-ed/contracts.

`@conform-ed/contracts` is a single package of runtime [Zod](https://zod.dev) validators and the TypeScript types inferred from them. Each standard lives behind its own versioned entry point, so you import only what you need.

```bash
bun add @conform-ed/contracts zod
```

## Validating a payload

[Section titled “Validating a payload”](#validating-a-payload)

Every standard is re-exported from the package root as a namespace, and is also available from a versioned subpath:

```ts
import { XapiV1_0_3 } from "@conform-ed/contracts/xapi/v1_0_3";


const result = XapiV1_0_3.Schemas.Statement.safeParse(payload);
if (!result.success) {
  console.error(result.error.issues);
}
```

Standards modelled from XSD/JSON-Schema entry points expose those entry points through a `…DerivedZodTemplates` object (for example `Qti301DerivedZodTemplates.qtiAssessmentItemDocument`).

Note

These models are deliberately *normalised* for ergonomic TypeScript use — they are faithful to the specification’s meaning but are not always a literal 1:1 of the XML/JSON binding. The [Coverage map](/coverage/) measures and documents exactly where conform-ed’s model differs from the literal published schema.

## By standard

[Section titled “By standard”](#by-standard)

| Standard                   | Page                                             | Entry point                                                 |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| xAPI 1.0.3 / IEEE 2.0      | [xAPI](/contracts/xapi/)                         | `@conform-ed/contracts/xapi/v1_0_3`, `…/xapi/v2_0`          |
| cmi5 (Quartz)              | [cmi5](/contracts/cmi5/)                         | `@conform-ed/contracts/cmi5/v1_0`                           |
| LTI 1.3 (+ companions)     | [LTI 1.3](/contracts/lti/)                       | `@conform-ed/contracts/lti/v1_3`                            |
| QTI 2.1 / 2.2 / 3.0.1      | [QTI](/contracts/qti/)                           | `@conform-ed/contracts/qti/v3_0_1` (and `…/v2_1`, `…/v2_2`) |
| Common Cartridge 1.3 / 1.4 | [Common Cartridge](/contracts/common-cartridge/) | `@conform-ed/contracts/common-cartridge/v1_3`, `…/v1_4`     |
| OneRoster 1.2              | [OneRoster](/contracts/oneroster/)               | `@conform-ed/contracts/oneroster/v1_2`                      |
| CASE 1.1                   | [CASE](/contracts/case/)                         | `@conform-ed/contracts/case/v1_1`                           |
| CLR 2.0                    | [CLR](/contracts/clr/)                           | `@conform-ed/contracts/clr/v2_0`                            |
| Open Badges 3.0            | [Open Badges](/contracts/open-badges/)           | `@conform-ed/contracts/open-badges/v3_0`                    |
| Caliper 1.2                | [Caliper](/contracts/caliper/)                   | `@conform-ed/contracts/caliper/v1_2`                        |
| CAT 1.0                    | [CAT](/contracts/cat/)                           | `@conform-ed/contracts/cat/v1_0`                            |
| VC Data Model 2.0          | [VC Data Model](/contracts/vc-data-model/)       | `@conform-ed/contracts/vc-data-model/v2_0`                  |
| h5p                        | [h5p](/contracts/h5p/)                           | `@conform-ed/contracts/h5p/v1`                              |
| ELM / EDC 3.3              | [ELM / EDC](/contracts/elm/)                     | `@conform-ed/contracts/elm/v3_3`                            |

# Caliper

> Caliper Analytics 1.2 envelope, event, and entity contracts.

Validators for Caliper Analytics 1.2 — the envelope, the full event and entity vocabularies, and the supporting metric/action/status vocabularies.

## Specification

[Section titled “Specification”](#specification)

* [Caliper v1.2](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/caliper/v1p2/impl/) — normative text and implementation guide, with the [CaliperBootcamp](https://github.com/1EdTech/CaliperBootcamp) v1.2 schema corpus used as the machine-readable compatibility source

## Import

[Section titled “Import”](#import)

```ts
import { Caliper12DerivedZodTemplates } from "@conform-ed/contracts/caliper/v1_2";


const result = Caliper12DerivedZodTemplates.envelope.safeParse(payload);
```

Namespace: `CaliperV1_2`. Entry points are exposed via `Caliper12DerivedZodTemplates` (`envelope`, `event`, `assessmentEvent`, `person`, `softwareApplication`, `session`, …).

## What’s modelled

[Section titled “What’s modelled”](#whats-modelled)

* The Caliper **envelope**, base event/entity contracts, and all Bootcamp v1.2 event and entity entry points.
* Full published Action/Profile/Metric/Status vocabularies, plus `SystemIdentifier`, `CaliperData`, and Selector helper schemas.
* An explicit source-precedence record (`CALIPER_REQUIREMENT_SOURCE_PRECEDENCE`) and `CaliperV1P2ConformanceMetadata` exposing the extracted textual rules.

## Validation notes

[Section titled “Validation notes”](#validation-notes)

* Event ids require URN UUID form (`urn:uuid:…`); datetimes require ISO 8601 UTC with millisecond precision; the envelope is strict and requires `sensor`, `sendTime`, `dataVersion`, and non-empty `data`.
* Actor/action/object subset constraints are enforced for the textual-spec-covered events (Assessment, Grade, Navigation, Session, …). Bootcamp-only events (Feedback, Outcome, Survey, …) are structurally validated but without that subset enforcement.
* Both `v1p1` and `v1p2` context URIs are accepted; per-entity property-table enforcement is not yet exhaustive (see the [Coverage map](/coverage/)).

# CASE

> CASE 1.1 competency and academic-standards framework contracts.

Validators for CASE 1.1 (Competency and Academic Standards Exchange) — frameworks, items, associations, and the supporting collection and status models.

## Specification

[Section titled “Specification”](#specification)

* [CASE v1.1](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/case/v1p1) — base spec, JSON Schema, and OpenAPI 3 definitions

## Import

[Section titled “Import”](#import)

```ts
import { CaseV1_1 } from "@conform-ed/contracts/case/v1_1";


const result = CaseV1_1.Schemas.CFPackage.safeParse(pkg);
```

Namespace: `CaseV1_1` (schemas under `.Schemas`).

## What’s modelled

[Section titled “What’s modelled”](#whats-modelled)

* **Core entities** — `CFPackage` (framework container), `CFItem` (competency/standard), `CFAssociation` (relationships), `CFRubric`, `CFDocument`, `CFLicense`.
* **Collections** — `CFAssociationSet`, `CFConceptSet`, `CFItemTypeSet`, `CFSubjectSet`, `CFDocumentSet`.
* **Integration** — IMSX status models and the OpenAPI 3 REST operation bindings (CRUD + retrieval).

## Validation notes

[Section titled “Validation notes”](#validation-notes)

* Source modules mirror the published JSON-schema artifact names, with shared validators in `shared.ts`.
* CASE is consumed and produced for real by conform-ed’s CASE provider/consumer; see the [Coverage map](/coverage/) for its conformance status.

# CAT

> Computer Adaptive Testing 1.0 REST service contracts.

Validators for the CAT 1.0 (Computer Adaptive Testing) core REST service contract and its shared payload types.

## Specification

[Section titled “Specification”](#specification)

* [CAT v1.0](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/cat/v1p0/impl/) — implementation guide and errata

## Import

[Section titled “Import”](#import)

```ts
import { CatV1_0 } from "@conform-ed/contracts/cat/v1_0";


const result = CatV1_0.Schemas.CreateSectionRequest.safeParse(payload);
```

Namespace: `CatV1_0` (schemas under `.Schemas`).

## What’s modelled

[Section titled “What’s modelled”](#whats-modelled)

* **Payload types** — `SectionData` (section configuration), `ItemStage` (item staging), `AssessmentResult` (candidate submission), `CatEngineResultReport` (engine return).
* **Operations** — request/response envelopes for `createSection`, `getSection`, `createSession`, `submitResults`, `endSession`, `endSection`, each exposing `method`, `path`, `requestPayload`, `successResponsePayload`, and `errorResponsePayload`.

## Validation notes

[Section titled “Validation notes”](#validation-notes)

* Payloads are strict objects; UUIDs use RFC 4122 validation; date-times accept both `Z` and numeric UTC offsets; enums allow `ext:*` extension tokens; errors normalize to `{ error, message, statusCode, details? }`.
* Engine-specific custom payload fragments are intentionally open (`z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())`) where the spec does not standardize vendor structures.

# CLR

> Comprehensive Learner Record 2.0 credential contracts.

Validators for CLR 2.0 (Comprehensive Learner Record), built on the shared verifiable-credential model it shares with Open Badges 3.0.

## Specification

[Section titled “Specification”](#specification)

* [CLR v2.0](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/clr/v2p0) — JSON Schema and JSON-LD credential model

## Import

[Section titled “Import”](#import)

```ts
import { Clr20DerivedZodTemplates } from "@conform-ed/contracts/clr/v2_0";


const result = Clr20DerivedZodTemplates.clrCredential.safeParse(credential);
```

Namespace: `ClrV2_0`. Published entry points are exposed via `Clr20DerivedZodTemplates` (`clrCredential`, `achievementCredential`, `endorsementCredential`, `getClrCredentialsResponse`, `profile`, `imsxStatusInfo`).

## What’s modelled

[Section titled “What’s modelled”](#whats-modelled)
